# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.117.0/http/server.ts";

function getModulePathsFromEnv(): string[] {
  const envPaths = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATHS");
  return envPaths ? envPaths.split(",") : [];
}

async function importModules(paths: string[]): Promise<void[]> {
  return Promise.all(paths.map(path => import(path)));
}

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const queryPaths = url.searchParams.getAll("module");
  
  const envPaths = getModulePathsFromEnv();
  const pathsToLoad = [...new Set([...envPaths, ...queryPaths])];
  
  try {
    await importModules(pathsToLoad);
    return new Response("Modules loaded successfully", { status: 200 });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error loading modules:", error);
    return new Response("Failed to load modules", { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve(handler);
